summary: Beyond The Ordinary Show is a free live webinar series hosted by John Burgos featuring 30+ spiritual thought leaders per season, used as the top-of-funnel lead engine for a paid community. Its core paid product is 'Living in the Extraordinary,' a $39/month month-to-month membership offering weekly live transmissions, Q&As, and direct access to John, with added revenue from a marketplace of speaker courses, audio pocket guides, and 1:1 mentorship. It serves spiritually curious adults (skewing women, 35–65) who feel stuck or unseen and are seeking clarity, transformation, and a community of kindred seekers.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive activation by getting a new free opt-in to show up to their first LIVE transmission — the felt 'aha' moment where content becomes transformation.
campaigns.trigger: Email opt-in for show replay access / free meditation download (send email 1 within seconds).
campaigns.segment: New free subscribers who have not yet attended a live call.
campaigns.rationale: The brand's entire conversion path depends on one event: getting a new subscriber to attend live, where John's presence does the closing. A replay link alone never delivers the aha, so onboarding must bridge 'I signed up for replays' to 'I showed up live and felt it.'

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations, and deliver the one action that creates the aha: claiming a seat on the next live transmission.
emails.subject: Welcome — your seat at the next live transmission is open
emails.preview: The replays are yours forever. But the shift happens live. Here's how to be in the room.
emails.cta: Save my seat for the next live call
keyPoints: Welcome them by name and honor why they came — clarity, connection, and a community that actually sees them
Reframe: replays are a gift, but the real shift happens live, in the room with John and the speakers
One single action — reserve a seat at the next live transmission, with the date/time and add-to-calendar
Set the rhythm: a few short heart-centered notes over the next two weeks, and a real human reply-to
Standard personal-results disclaimer kept light and on-brand

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture (Free → Membership)
campaigns.objective: Warm the large pool of under-monetized free subscribers toward 'Living in the Extraordinary' membership by building trust and resolving the free-vs-paid objection.
campaigns.trigger: Replay opt-in / meditation download with no membership purchase; lead below sales-ready threshold.
campaigns.segment: Engaged free subscribers (opened/attended at least once) who are not yet members; branch by the speaker or topic they engaged with.
campaigns.rationale: The free list is the brand's biggest under-tapped asset, and the #1 blocker to revenue is the question 'why pay $39 when so much is free?' This sequence's hero send answers that head-on without breaking the generous, non-salesy voice.

emails.purpose: Objection handling — make the honest case for why members pay $39/month when the show is free, framed as the difference between consuming and being held.
emails.subject: Why we pay $39 a month when the show is free
emails.preview: It's an honest question. Here's the real difference between watching and being held.
emails.cta: See what's inside Living in the Extraordinary
keyPoints: Name the objection openly and without defensiveness — it's the right question to ask
Draw the distinction: the free show is where you discover; the membership is where you're held weekly and pressured (lovingly) to actually change
Concrete contrast — weekly live transmissions, direct Q&A with John, on-demand library, and a community that holds you accountable
Member voice that mirrors success language: 'I finally feel like I belong somewhere' / 'I went from knowing to doing'
Lower the risk: $39/month, no contract, no catch — leave anytime

campaigns.name: Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize live attendance — the activation and revenue-proximate event — by pre-framing and reminding registered attendees so they actually show up.
campaigns.trigger: Seat reserved for an upcoming live transmission; upcoming-call cadence; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone registered for an upcoming live show or member transmission.
campaigns.rationale: Live attendance is both the aha and the closer for this brand, yet show-up rates are likely low without a pre-frame sequence; reminders cut no-shows ~35% on average, recovering attendance (and conversions) immediately.

emails.purpose: Day-of 'starting soon' reminder that builds anticipation and removes every barrier to clicking in live.
emails.subject: We're live today at {time} — your link is inside
emails.preview: Today's transmission with {speaker}. Come as you are — just bring an open heart.
keyPoints: Direct join link front and center — one tap to be in the room
Pre-frame the experience: today's speaker, the theme, and the felt shift to expect
Lower the bar to show up — no prep, no camera required, just be present for 60–90 minutes
Gentle urgency: this one is live and won't feel the same on replay
Reassure that a replay follows, but the energy and Q&A only happen live

campaigns.name: Free → Paid Membership Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a warm free attendee who has felt the live aha into a paying 'Living in the Extraordinary' member.
campaigns.trigger: Subscriber attended a live transmission (activation event) and is not yet a member.
campaigns.segment: Free subscribers who have attended at least one live call; branch by engagement depth.
campaigns.rationale: Once someone has felt the shift live, that resonance fades fast without a clear, low-friction path to make it a weekly practice — this is the moment of peak intent to convert free trust into recurring revenue.

emails.purpose: Strike while the resonance is fresh — invite the recent live attendee to make the experience their weekly practice inside the membership.
emails.subject: You felt something on that call. Let's make it weekly.
emails.preview: That shift you felt isn't a one-time thing. It's a practice — and it has a home.
emails.cta: Join Living in the Extraordinary — $39/mo, no contract
keyPoints: Reflect back the felt experience of the live call they just attended — name the resonance and shift
Position membership as the way to keep that feeling alive every week rather than chasing it season to season
What they get: weekly live transmissions, direct Q&A and access to John, the full replay library, and the community container
Address the lingering doubt — real, lasting change comes from consistency and accountability, not more free content
Low-friction close: $39/month, month-to-month, cancel anytime, plus a human reply-to for questions

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant free subscribers and churned members, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: 60–90 days of no opens/clicks/live attendance, or membership cancellation.
campaigns.segment: Dormant free subscribers; separately, churned members (different, warmer message referencing what they had).
campaigns.rationale: Lapsed free opt-ins and quietly-cancelled members are not being reactivated, and re-engagement recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts while sunsetting the rest keeps sender reputation healthy.

emails.purpose: Heart-centered 'we miss you' that reconnects them to the core longing that brought them in and invites them back to one live call.
emails.subject: Did the noise pull you back under?
emails.preview: No guilt, no pitch. Just an open door back to the room whenever you're ready.
emails.cta: Come back to the next live transmission
keyPoints: Open with empathy, not guilt — life and noise pull all of us away from the deeper work
Remind them of why they came: clarity over confusion, connection over feeling unseen
A warm, no-pressure invitation back to a single upcoming live transmission
For the churned-member branch, reference what they valued most and what's new since they left
Sets up the later sunset email — if this re-opens nothing, ask whether to stop emailing to protect their inbox and our deliverability
